titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the David I. Wells papers, 1964-1980.

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library

profiledesc.creation: Finding aid encoded by Martin Heggestad, May 2004

titlepage.titleproper: Guide to the David I. Wells Papers,1964-1980.
titlepage.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections Cornell University Library

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu

did.head: DESCRIPTIVE SUMMARY
did.unittitle: David I. Wells papers,
did.physdesc: Correspondence, subject files, legal briefs and papers, and other material.
did.repository: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library
did.abstract: Correspondence, subject files, legal briefs and papers, and other material pertaining to Wells' career and interest in legislative representation and redistricting, including the Wells Vs. Rockefeller Supreme Court challenge, anti-gerrymandering legislation, presidential election system reform, and other legal cases concerning districting and reform.

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Lecturer and writer on matters of apportionment.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, subject files, legal briefs and papers, and other material pertaining to Wells' career and interest in legislative representation and redistricting, including the Wells Vs. Rockefeller Supreme Court challenge, anti-gerrymandering legislation, presidential election system reform, and other legal cases concerning districting and reform.

did.unittitle: D.W. written materials (several folders)
D.W. written materials (several folders)
Correspondence to D.W.
Correspondence by D.W.
Articles and letters mentioning D.W.
Miscellaneous D.W. activities and involvements
Legislative Representation in New York State ("LRNYS") - a booklet by D.W., issued in several editions between 1957 and 1964 (2 folders)
General material on redistricting reform (mostly late 1970s, early 1980s)
WMCA case (WMCA v. Lomenzo). Case which, when consolidated with cases from five other states, became the so-called "one person-one vote" Supreme Court ruling of 1964 (3 folders)
New York State Committee for Fair Representation ("NYSCFR") - 2 folders (successful)
National Committee for Fair Representation ("NCFR")
"Black-Republican Redistricting Alliance"
Race and redistricting, post-1980
Schwartz case, 1971-1972 (subsequently combined with Schnider v. Rockefeller) - attacking constitutionalityof New York State legislative redistricting of 1971-1972 (4 folders)
1974 N.Y.S. racial redistricting ruling, leading to United Jewish Organizations (UJO) v. Carey
Proposed federal anti-gerrymandering legislation
D.W. consultation work (national), initiated through AFL-CIO COPE (post-1970 redistricting) - 2 folders
Informal Advisory Committee on Redistricting to Democratic National Committee (DNC)
State legislative redistricting (national),
Local redistricting
DW, GT (Gus Tyler) and other's writing on redistricting - early
Articles on redistricting, post-
Computer redistricting, post-
Congressional redistricting (national),
Legal briefs on redistricting (most early 1970 s)
Legal decisions on redistricting (most early 1970 s)
Presidential election system reform; the Electoral College
Presidential election system reform; the Electoral College
Summaries of events & cases, post- 1970 redistricting
1992 N.Y.S. congressional redistricting, including D.W. plan submitted to task force
State-by-state files on redistricting, in alphabetical order, beginning with this box which runs from Alabama through Georgia

scopecontent.p: State files contain various materials on redistricting in the state in general, as well as some correspondence and other material on D.W. involvements in the state. Note, for example, Colorado, where League of Women Voters successfully campaigned to get proposal drafted in part by D.W. onto the ballot through initiative process. Proposal passed (1974) and was incorporated into state constitution.

did.unittitle: State-by-state files, continued (Hawaii through Missouri)

scopecontent.p: NOTE: Pre-1990 files for twelve states (Idaho through Minnesota, alphabetically) are missing.

did.unittitle: State-by-state files, continued (Montana through New Jersey)

scopecontent.p: NOTE: New Jersey files include extensive material on D.W. involvement in N.J. congressional redistricting in early 1970s at initial request of Congressman (and House Judiciary Committee Chairman) Peter Rodino.

did.unittitle: State-by-state files, continued (New Mexico through Oklahoma)
State-by-state files, continued (Oregon through Vermont)
State-by-state files, continued (Virginia through Wyoming, plus Canada, France, Japan, etc.)

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: David I. Wells papers, #4917. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
